# Handoff — Full Roadmap: content-materialization program (EPIC 3be95a71)

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend
- `<memory-api>` -> [./memory-api](./memory-api) — owning ticket store for EPIC and all `G-*` children (query with `workspace=<memory-api>`; note `get` against `default` fails for these ids, aggregated `subgraph`/`list` include them).
- `<root>` -> [./](./) — root context-engine ticket store (holds the done URN/discovery foundation tickets).
- `EPIC` / `G-*` / `HEALTH-PROMOTE` / `XSTORE` / `GUARD` / `SESSION-EPIC` — see Ticket Legend.

## Ticket Legend
- `EPIC` -> 3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c — "[content-materialization][epic] Fill specs, Rust/code policy, and the feedback ring on top of session construction" (effort 8000, new)
- `G-A` -> 633a38b8-7200-4979-9ce9-8c296ccc78bd — "[content-materialization][spec-api] G-A: Spec-contract v2 — motivation, dependent-expectation, computed guards, positions" (effort 2500, new; depends_on HEALTH-PROMOTE)
- `G-B` -> 17483930-36a1-4bf7-864b-e30ded445db3 — "[content-materialization][rule-api] G-B: Rust code-design policy — typed errors, trait contracts, trait inheritance" (effort 2000, new; depends_on G-B.1, G-B.2)
- `G-B.1` -> 7c281aec-092c-448d-bf26-94522e07b4e8 — "[content-materialization][memory-api] G-B.1 retro-fix: typed MoveError variant for interoperability-contract violation (replace string marker)" (effort 1500, new; leaf — smallest fully-specified)
- `G-B.2` -> fa6e2c19-5fd8-4305-90c7-f1f4bea8df36 — "[content-materialization][memory-api] G-B.2 retro-fix: trait-based interoperability contract (replace runtime validate_interoperability_contract)" (effort 2500, new; leaf)
- `G-C` -> 6875dff6-7191-46aa-9358-9567430969f1 — "[content-materialization][rule-api] G-C: Rule-introduces-spec — status-conditioned spec presentation in session construction" (effort 1800, new; leaf, no prereq)
- `G-D` -> 5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d — "[content-materialization][feedback-api] G-D: Close the feedback ring — execution→verified, transcript mining, missing-rule tickets, ticket-entity feedback" (effort 3000, new; NEEDS-DETAIL — carries no depends_on edges)
- `G-E` -> 7f7ef435-0504-40cf-8d6c-dd96477a0223 — "[content-materialization][workspace] G-E: De-submodularize — real dependency imports instead of nested submodules (gated)" (effort 3500, new; depends_on G-A, G-B, G-C, G-D)
- `HEALTH-PROMOTE` -> c680b137-01a1-4d13-b219-39af86eaa71b — "[ticket-api] Promote health_check to a ticket-api call with CLI/MCP/HTTP transport parity" (effort 2500, new; depends_on GUARD[done], XSTORE)
- `XSTORE` -> f3a58d3c-5961-4ee4-a32d-b51343a5a275 — "[memory-api] Cross-store edge health: shared resolver + policy-aware parent-workspace warning (parity across all stores)" (effort 3500, READY — groomed this session; unblocked leaf)
- `GUARD` -> 835b332b-8c1f-4423-bf21-4fac7aa7c8f7 — "[ticket-api] Health + state-guard: stop warning on ready→ready; flag/guard only tickets progressed ahead of their dependencies" (effort 1200, DONE)
- `SESSION-EPIC` -> effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8 — "[session-bootstrap][epic] Dynamic session bootstrapping & context routing redesign" (new; large parallel sub-program EPIC depends on)
- Foundation (all DONE, `<root>` store): 82d6ada4-ac35-45a7-9df6-7b7501d58e70 (URN model+resolver), 6bd67a7a-2a76-4dd7-a897-b4d325476621 (multi-store discovery), 7e318b2a-a381-49a1-aee9-18758a4b80fd (late-store reconciliation)
- Related (context): b03be2d5-5293-4dc7-ad11-cca2dbf32c8b (memory-api cross-entity edges), 7599ed31-598e-458e-8651-9bfe6c57ffd9 (placement principle), 671d4e47-b53d-4a04-aa1d-30f2aa8a2bbe (multi-store architecture tracker), b1e9e744-aeac-474a-91d9-07e3a362dc76 (feedback-api inbox tracker), 8a90a63c-0a07-439f-90e8-9124212b2dc8 (new-store-domains umbrella)

## Overview
EPIC 3be95a71 layers four content tracks (spec-contract v2, Rust code-design policy, rule-introduces-spec, feedback-ring closure) plus a gated de-submodularization on top of the session-bootstrap program, unblocked by a ticket-api health/cross-store cleanup pair. This handoff is the full roadmap: the program is structurally sound (edge directions correct, descriptions carry real motivation/acceptance), GUARD is done, XSTORE is freshly re-scoped to base memory-api and ready, and G-B.1 is the smallest fully-specified starting node. Start from `<memory-api>`.

## Findings
- Program shape: EPIC depends on 8 tracks — G-A, G-B, G-C, G-D, G-E, HEALTH-PROMOTE, XSTORE, SESSION-EPIC. Total direct-track effort ≈ 22,800 (excluding EPIC rollup 8000 and the SESSION-EPIC subtree).
- Unblocked leaves (start now): XSTORE (ready), G-B.1 (new), G-B.2 (new), G-C (new). GUARD is already done.
- Health-unblock chain: HEALTH-PROMOTE depends on GUARD (done) + XSTORE; with GUARD done, HEALTH-PROMOTE waits only on XSTORE. G-A depends on HEALTH-PROMOTE. So XSTORE → HEALTH-PROMOTE → G-A is the health-gated spine.
- G-B rolls up G-B.1 + G-B.2 (both memory-api retro-fixes replacing string-marker/runtime interoperability validation with typed + trait contracts).
- G-E is the terminal gate: depends on G-A, G-B, G-C, G-D — do last.
- G-D is the one under-specified node: description references feedback-api b1e9e744-aeac-474a-91d9-07e3a362dc76 + umbrella 8a90a63c-0a07-439f-90e8-9124212b2dc8 but carries NO depends_on edges. Left unlinked deliberately (blind cross-subtree linking would spawn cross-store danglers before XSTORE lands).
- XSTORE was re-scoped this session from ticket-api-only into a base-memory-api shared classifier (OK / `cross_workspace_edge` warning / `dangling_edge` error) with parity across ticket+spec+test+rule+audit; foundation (82d6ada4/6bd67a7a/7e318b2a) is done so it is not blocked.
- Cross-store danglers on 82d6ada4 / 6bd67a7a / f8b447b7 remain benign until XSTORE lands — do NOT prune.

## Decisions
- Sequence health spine first (XSTORE → HEALTH-PROMOTE) so subtree health becomes trustworthy before the content tracks close.
- Recommend G-B.1 as the first hands-on implementation node (smallest, exact file targets, zero reconstruction); XSTORE if you prefer to clear the health signal first.
- Keep G-D's cross-subtree links textual (not hard edges) until XSTORE resolves cross-store references.
- EPIC stays in `<memory-api>`; deferred cluster moves to memory-api happen only after XSTORE + move tooling, not now.

## Blockers
- No hard blockers for the unblocked leaves. Foundation is complete.
- G-D is soft-blocked on grooming (missing acceptance detail + intentional absence of edges); resolve before implementing it.
- G-A cannot start until HEALTH-PROMOTE lands (which needs XSTORE).
- G-E cannot start until G-A/G-B/G-C/G-D are done.

## Long-Horizon Goal (EPIC)
3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c reaches done: specs carry v2 contract fields, the Rust code-design policy is enforced, rule-introduces-spec presentation works in session construction, the feedback ring is closed, de-submodularization is complete, and store health is clean under the new cross-store classifier.

## Next Actions (execution order)
1. Implement XSTORE f3a58d3c-5961-4ee4-a32d-b51343a5a275 — base-memory-api shared cross-store edge classifier (OK / cross_workspace_edge warning / dangling error), ticket+spec health delegation. First validation: `cargo test -p ticket-api --lib` in [./memory-api](./memory-api) (expect prior-green minus the one documented unrelated move_planner failure) + a new four-case classifier test (descendant→OK, ancestor→warning, unresolvable→error, ticket/spec parity); confirm memory-api-scoped health drops from 5 danglers to 0 errors.
2. Implement HEALTH-PROMOTE c680b137-01a1-4d13-b219-39af86eaa71b — promote health_check to a ticket-api call with CLI/MCP/HTTP parity (delegating transports to canonical `collect_findings`). Validation: transport-parity tests + `cargo test -p ticket-api`.
3. Implement G-B.1 7c281aec-092c-448d-bf26-94522e07b4e8 then G-B.2 fa6e2c19-5fd8-4305-90c7-f1f4bea8df36 (memory-api typed MoveError + trait interoperability contract), then close G-B 17483930-36a1-4bf7-864b-e30ded445db3.
4. Implement G-C 6875dff6-7191-46aa-9358-9567430969f1 (rule-introduces-spec; independent leaf — can run in parallel with steps 1–3).
5. Implement G-A 633a38b8-7200-4979-9ce9-8c296ccc78bd (spec-contract v2) once HEALTH-PROMOTE is done.
6. Groom then implement G-D 5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d (feedback ring): add acceptance detail and, after XSTORE lands, wire its real dependency edges.
7. Implement G-E 7f7ef435-0504-40cf-8d6c-dd96477a0223 (de-submodularize) last, once G-A/G-B/G-C/G-D are done.
8. Coordinate SESSION-EPIC effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8 in parallel (session-api substream EPIC also depends on); it does not block the content tracks above.
9. Close EPIC 3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c once all tracks are done and health is clean.

## Completion Status
1. XSTORE implementation — not-started (ticket READY, groomed this session)
2. HEALTH-PROMOTE — not-started (blocked on step 1; GUARD dep already done)
3. G-B.1 / G-B.2 / G-B — not-started (G-B.1 is the recommended first hands-on node)
4. G-C — not-started (independent leaf)
5. G-A — not-started (blocked on step 2)
6. G-D — blocked (needs grooming: missing acceptance detail + edges)
7. G-E — not-started (blocked on G-A/G-B/G-C/G-D)
8. SESSION-EPIC coordination — in-progress (design afa00b5c + core gate c7542933 done; runtime/cli/cascade tracks open)
9. Close EPIC — not-started

## Definition Of Done
EPIC 3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c is done; every G-* track and HEALTH-PROMOTE/XSTORE are done; store health reports zero dangling errors under the new classifier with parent-workspace edges surfaced as warnings; the feedback ring is closed and session construction consumes the materialized specs/rules; validation suites pass except the documented unrelated `storage::move_planner::tests::preflight_reports_invisible_reference_visibility_and_path_refs` failure.

## Key Entities
- [EPIC ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c/ticket.toml) — program root and rollup.
- [XSTORE ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/f3a58d3c-5961-4ee4-a32d-b51343a5a275/ticket.toml) — freshly re-scoped base-memory-api classifier; the health spine's first node.
- [HEALTH-PROMOTE ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/c680b137-01a1-4d13-b219-39af86eaa71b/ticket.toml) — transport-parity health call; gates G-A.
- [G-B.1 ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/7c281aec-092c-448d-bf26-94522e07b4e8/ticket.toml) — recommended first hands-on node.
- [G-B.2 ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/fa6e2c19-5fd8-4305-90c7-f1f4bea8df36/ticket.toml), [G-B ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/17483930-36a1-4bf7-864b-e30ded445db3/ticket.toml) — Rust code-design policy rollup.
- [G-A ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/633a38b8-7200-4979-9ce9-8c296ccc78bd/ticket.toml), [G-C ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6875dff6-7191-46aa-9358-9567430969f1/ticket.toml), [G-D ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d/ticket.toml), [G-E ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/7f7ef435-0504-40cf-8d6c-dd96477a0223/ticket.toml) — the four content tracks + terminal gate.
- [GUARD ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/835b332b-8c1f-4423-bf21-4fac7aa7c8f7/ticket.toml) — done; state-guard + health fix already shipped.
- Foundation (done): [82d6ada4](./.ticket/tickets/82d6ada4-ac35-45a7-9df6-7b7501d58e70/ticket.toml) URN resolver, [6bd67a7a](./.ticket/tickets/6bd67a7a-2a76-4dd7-a897-b4d325476621/ticket.toml) discovery, [7e318b2a](./.ticket/tickets/7e318b2a-a381-49a1-aee9-18758a4b80fd/ticket.toml) reconciliation.
- Base code the XSTORE/HEALTH work builds on: [urn.rs](./memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/model/urn.rs) (`Urn`/`UrnResolver`), [discovery.rs](./memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/discovery.rs) (`discover_stores`), [workspace_policy.rs](./memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace_policy.rs), [health.rs](./memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/health.rs) (`collect_findings`, `append_dangling_edge_findings`).
- Related planning: [b03be2d5](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b03be2d5-5293-4dc7-ad11-cca2dbf32c8b/ticket.toml) cross-entity edges (coordination note added), [7599ed31](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/7599ed31-598e-458e-8651-9bfe6c57ffd9/ticket.toml) placement principle, [SESSION-EPIC](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8/ticket.toml).

## Board / Persisted-Session Notes
- No active board entries required. Check in on the specific ticket you start (XSTORE or G-B.1) before editing files.
- Reads: use `workspace=<memory-api>` for `get`/`get_ticket_description`; `default` aggregates for `subgraph`/`list` but `get` by id fails against `default` for these tickets.
- Commit deepest-first, owned files only; exclude `Cargo.lock`, `.session/`, and the dirty `memory-viewers` submodule. Keep the documented `move_planner` preflight failure flagged as unrelated — never silently absorbed.

Execute the next actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.

Avoid adding implicit test dependency usage by writing workspace policy TOML manually in ticket health test.
arguments.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: c:\Users\linus\git\graph_app\context-engine\memory-api\crates\ticket-api\src\health.rs
@@
-        let policy = WorkspacePolicy {
-            include_descendants: true,
-            include_ancestors: false,
-            deny_external_paths: false,
-            ..WorkspacePolicy::default()
-        };
         fs::write(
             child_repo
                 .join(".ticket")
                 .join(WORKSPACE_POLICY_FILE),
-            toml::to_string(&policy).unwrap(),
+            "include_descendants = true\ninclude_ancestors = false\ndeny_external_paths = false\n",
         )
         .unwrap();
*** End Patch

arguments.explanation: Fix classifier to respect the passed WorkspacePolicy by using policy-aware scan-root discovery rather than loading policy from disk implicitly.
arguments.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: c:\Users\linus\git\graph_app\context-engine\memory-api\crates\memory-api\src\cross_store_edges.rs
@@
     workspace::{
-        discover_workspace_store_roots,
+        discover_workspace_scan_roots_with_policy,
         resolve_store_root_from,
         resolve_workspace_root_from_store_root,
     },
@@
-        let mut included_store_roots = discover_workspace_store_roots(
-            &active_workspace_root,
-            layout.store_dir,
-            layout.entity_dir,
-        );
+        let mut included_store_roots =
+            discover_workspace_scan_roots_with_policy(
+                &active_workspace_root,
+                layout.store_dir,
+                layout.entity_dir,
+                &policy,
+            )
+            .into_iter()
+            .map(|root| resolve_store_root_from(&root.path, layout.store_dir))
+            .collect::<Vec<_>>();
*** End Patch

@@
 pub struct HealthArgs {
     /// Root ticket UUID or 8+ character hex prefix. Checks the subgraph rooted here.
-    #[arg(required_unless_present_any = ["all", "stdin"])]
+    #[arg(required_unless_present_any = ["all", "stdin", "ids"])]
     pub root: Option<String>,
@@
     /// Read newline-delimited ticket UUIDs from stdin instead of traversing a subgraph.
     #[arg(long, default_value_t = false)]
     pub stdin: bool,
+    /// Explicit ticket IDs (UUID or 8+ prefix). Can be repeated.
+    #[arg(long = "id")]
+    pub ids: Vec<String>,
*** End Patch

*** Update File: c:\Users\linus\git\graph_app\context-engine\memory-api\crates\rule-api\src\move_domain.rs
@@
 fn from_move_error(error: MoveError) -> RuleError {
     match error {
         MoveError::Io(io) => RuleError::Storage(StorageError::Io(io)),
         MoveError::Domain(message) =>
             RuleError::Storage(StorageError::Other(message)),
+        MoveError::InteroperabilityContract {
+            artifact_class,
+            detail,
+        } => RuleError::Storage(StorageError::Other(format!(
+            "interoperability contract violation for {artifact_class}: {detail}"
+        ))),
     }
 }
*** End Patch

data_json.content:
Implemented the roadmap in order through XSTORE, HEALTH-PROMOTE, and G-B.1, with ticket/board state updates and validation.

**Completed now**
1. XSTORE f3a58d3c is implemented and moved to in-review.
2. HEALTH-PROMOTE c680b137 is implemented and moved to in-review.
3. G-B.1 7c281aec is implemented and moved to in-review.
4. All three were checked in/out on the board with handoff reasons recorded.

**What was changed**
1. Added shared cross-store edge classifier in memory-api:
- [crates/memory-api/src/cross_store_edges.rs](crates/memory-api/src/cross_store_edges.rs)
- Exported from [crates/memory-api/src/lib.rs](crates/memory-api/src/lib.rs)

2. Ticket health now delegates unresolved depends_on edges to shared classifier and emits cross_workspace_edge warning vs dangling_edge error:
- [crates/ticket-api/src/health.rs](crates/ticket-api/src/health.rs)

3. Spec health now applies same cross-store classification for depends_on edges:
- [crates/spec-api/src/store.rs](crates/spec-api/src/store.rs)

4. Added parity-focused tests:
- [crates/spec-api/src/store/tests.rs](crates/spec-api/src/store/tests.rs)
- [crates/ticket-api/src/health.rs](crates/ticket-api/src/health.rs)
- [crates/memory-api/src/cross_store_edges.rs](crates/memory-api/src/cross_store_edges.rs)

5. Promoted health-check transport parity parameters (root, ids, all, depth, direction, where/filter):
- CLI args + handling:
  - [tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli/args/operations.rs](tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli/args/operations.rs)
  - [tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli/commands/ops/health.rs](tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli/commands/ops/health.rs)
- MCP input + execution:
  - [tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/types.rs](tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/types.rs)
  - [tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server.rs](tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server.rs)
  - [tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/health.rs](tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/health.rs)
  - [tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/workflow.rs](tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/workflow.rs)
- HTTP query + execution:
  - [tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/graph.rs](tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/graph.rs)
  - [tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/graph/quality.rs](tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/graph/quality.rs)
  - [tools/http/ticket-http/tests/integration_parity/tests.rs](tools/http/ticket-http/tests/integration_parity/tests.rs)

6. G-B.1 typed MoveError retrofit:
- Added typed variant for interoperability contract violations:
  - [crates/memory-api/src/storage/move_kernel_types.rs](crates/memory-api/src/storage/move_kernel_types.rs)
- Updated kernel contract test expectations:
  - [crates/memory-api/src/storage/move_kernel/internal.rs](crates/memory-api/src/storage/move_kernel/internal.rs)
- Updated all requested adapters to handle new variant:
  - [crates/ticket-api/src/storage/move_planner.rs](crates/ticket-api/src/storage/move_planner.rs)
  - [crates/spec-api/src/move_domain.rs](crates/spec-api/src/move_domain.rs)
  - [crates/rule-api/src/move_domain.rs](crates/rule-api/src/move_domain.rs)
  - [crates/session-api/src/move_domain.rs](crates/session-api/src/move_domain.rs)
  - [crates/audit-api/src/move_domain.rs](crates/audit-api/src/move_domain.rs)

**Validation run**
1. Passed:
- cargo test -p memory-api cross_store_edges
- cargo test -p ticket-api health::tests::cross_workspace_dependency_is_warning_not_dangling_error
- cargo test -p spec-api health_reports_cross_workspace_and_dangling_depends_on_edges
- cargo test -p ticket-http health_findings_parity_across_http_and_mcp
- cargo test -p ticket-cli --lib
- cargo test -p ticket-mcp --lib
- cargo check across touched crates:

data_json.content:
I've completed a full review with a verification of the real workspace config. There is one blocking defect that the test suite masks.

## Review verdict — content-materialization slice (`f3a58d3c`, `c680b137`, `7c281aec`)

Overall the structure is good and G-B.1 is clean, but **`f3a58d3c` does not meet its core acceptance criterion under the real memory-api policy.** Requesting changes.

### 🔴 Blocker 1 — ancestor resolution is gated on `!deny_external_paths`, so the real false positives are NOT fixed

The classifier only adds ancestor stores to `discoverable_store_roots` when `deny_external_paths == false`:

- cross_store_edges.rs — `if !policy.deny_external_paths { for ancestor in ... }`
- and `included_store_roots` also excludes ancestors when denied — workspace.rs — `if policy.include_ancestors && !policy.deny_external_paths`

But the actual production policy is `deny_external_paths = true`:
- workspace-policy.toml → `include_ancestors = true`, `deny_external_paths = true`
- The original 5 danglers are memory-api tickets → **root-store** targets `82d6ada4` / `6bd67a7a` / `f8b447b7`, and root is memory-api's **ancestor**.

Under the real config, the ancestor root store lands in **neither** `included_store_roots` **nor** `discoverable_store_roots`, so those edges classify as `EdgeReferenceResolution::DanglingEdge` → recorded as a `dangling_edge` **error** — exactly the false positive the ticket set out to eliminate. The acceptance criterion "memory-api-scoped health drops from 5 danglers to 0 errors" is unmet, and the user's requested `cross_workspace_edge` warning is never produced in any real store.

Root cause: `deny_external_paths` is an **indexing** boundary; it's being reused to gate **health classification**. The user's directive was explicitly "discourage edges to parent workspaces *if the policy suggests not indexing them* — this should be a health warning." That is precisely the `deny_external_paths = true` case, so gating resolution off in that case disables the feature exactly when it's needed.

**Fix:** decouple resolution from `deny_external_paths`. The classifier should resolve ancestor stores for *classification* regardless of the indexing boundary, and emit `CrossWorkspaceEdge` (warning) when the target resolves only in a non-indexed ancestor. Keep `deny_external_paths` meaning "don't index," not "don't classify."

### 🔴 Blocker 2 — every test forces `deny_external_paths = false`, hiding Blocker 1

All four classifier tests plus the ticket/spec health tests set `deny_external_paths: false` or use `compatibility_default()` (which is `false`):
- cross_store_edges.rs, health.rs, store/tests.rs

No test exercises the production default (`deny_external_paths = true`). Add a regression test: ancestor edge under `deny_external_paths = true` must still produce `cross_workspace_edge` (post-fix). That test would have caught Blocker 1.

### 🟡 Quality — `c680b137` adds duplication, contrary to "promote to a single ticket-api call"

The cross-store classifier is nicely centralized, but the transport work re-duplicates logic instead of promoting it:
- `parse_where_filters` + `apply_field_filters` are **copy-pasted verbatim** in both ticket-http quality.rs and ticket-mcp health.rs.
- `apply_field_filters` re-implements ticket-api's existing `matches_field_filters`; HTTP `resolve_uuid_prefix` re-implements MCP's `resolve_uuid_with`.
- Extract these into ticket-api and have both transports delegate — that's the actual "parity via one call" the ticket title promises.
- Also note `apply_field_filters` only supports `state`/`type`/`title`, a weaker subset than `list_extended`'s arbitrary field filters (consistent HTTP↔MCP, but a parity gap vs CLI).

### 🟢 Looks good
- **G-B.1 (`7c281aec`)** is clean: typed `MoveError::InteroperabilityContract { artifact_class, detail }` replaces the string marker, `INTEROP_CONTRACT_MARKER` removed, all five domain adapters (ticket/spec/rule/session/audit) and both contract tests updated. Correct and on-policy. Minor: the variant is flattened back to strings at each domain boundary — fine for this slice, a fuller G-B pass could add typed domain variants.
- Centralized 3-way `EdgeReferenceResolution` enum; ticket + spec both delegate; parity test present.
- Security: paths built from validated UUIDs (no traversal); where-clauses exact-match only (no injection). No OWASP concerns.
- Minor: dead `let _ = root;` in cross_store_edges.rs — use `.any(...)`.

### Recommendation
Keep all three tickets in `in-review`. `7c281aec` (G-B.1) is mergeable on its own. `f3a58d3c` needs Blockers 1–2 resolved before it can move to `done`; `c680b137` should fold the duplicated helpers into ticket-api.

data_json.content:
Implemented your requested changes for both blockers and the de-dup quality gap.

What I changed

1. Fixed blocker 1: ancestor classification now works when deny_external_paths = true
- Removed the deny_external_paths gate around ancestor store discovery in [memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/cross_store_edges.rs](memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/cross_store_edges.rs).
- This decouples health classification from indexing boundaries, so ancestor targets can classify as cross_workspace_edge warnings even when indexing is denied.
- Also removed the dead code pattern you flagged (replaced find + ignored binding with any).

2. Fixed blocker 2: production-policy regression coverage added
- Updated classifier regression expectations in [memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/cross_store_edges.rs](memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/cross_store_edges.rs) to use include_ancestors = true and deny_external_paths = true.
- Updated ticket health regression to the same production-like policy in [memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/health.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/health.rs).
- Updated spec health regression to the same production-like policy in [memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/store/tests.rs](memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/store/tests.rs).

3. Addressed c680 quality issue: removed HTTP/MCP duplication by delegating to ticket-api helpers
- Added shared helper module [memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/query_helpers.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/query_helpers.rs):
  - parse_where_filters
  - apply_field_filters (now supports arbitrary manifest string fields, not just state/type/title)
  - resolve_uuid_with_prefix
- Exported it via [memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/lib.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/lib.rs).
- Rewired HTTP health endpoint to use shared helpers in [memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/graph/quality.rs](memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/graph/quality.rs).
- Rewired MCP health endpoint to use shared helpers in [memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/health.rs](memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/health.rs).
- Rewired MCP UUID resolution to shared helper in [memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server.rs](memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server.rs).

Validation run

- Passed: cargo test -p memory-api cross_store_edges
- Passed: cargo test -p ticket-api cross_workspace_dependency_is_warning_not_dangling_error
- Passed: cargo test -p spec-api health_reports_cross_workspace_and_dangling_depends_on_edges
- Passed: cargo test -p ticket-http health_findings_parity_across_http_and_mcp
- Passed: cargo test -p ticket-mcp --lib

Review status delta

- f3a58d3c blocker is now resolved under the real policy shape.
- c680b137 duplication concern was addressed by centralizing helper logic in ticket-api and delegating both HTTP and MCP.
- 7c281aec remains unaffected and clean.

data_json.content:
Re-review complete. Both blockers are genuinely resolved; the de-dup is done except one residual I want to flag.

## Re-review verdict

### ✅ Blocker 1 — resolved
The `deny_external_paths` gate around ancestor discovery is gone; the ancestor loop in cross_store_edges.rs is now unconditional. I traced classification across all policy shapes and it's correct:
- `include_ancestors=true, deny_external_paths=false` → ancestor is in `included_store_roots` → **Ok** (no false warning).
- `include_ancestors=true, deny_external_paths=true` (real memory-api) → suppressed from `included` by workspace.rs but added to `discoverable` → **CrossWorkspaceEdge warning**. The 5 root-store danglers now become warnings, not errors — acceptance criterion met.
- unresolvable → **DanglingEdge**. Dead `let _ = root;` replaced with `.any(...)`. Good.

### ✅ Blocker 2 — resolved
The classifier and ticket/spec health regressions now use the production shape `include_ancestors=true, deny_external_paths=true` and assert the ancestor edge yields `cross_workspace_edge` — cross_store_edges.rs. This is the guard that would have caught Blocker 1.

### ✅ De-dup — done (HTTP + MCP)
New query_helpers.rs centralizes the three helpers; HTTP quality.rs and MCP health.rs + server.rs all delegate. Bonus: `apply_field_filters` now reads arbitrary manifest fields (matching `list_extended`), and `resolve_uuid_with_prefix` gained hex validation + case-insensitive `simple()` matching. Solid upgrade over the copy-paste.

### 🟡 Minor residual — CLI still has a third copy
health.rs keeps its own `apply_field_filters` + `matches_filters`, still limited to `state`/`type`/`title`. Two consequences:
- a third duplicate the centralization missed, and
- a re-introduced parity gap: CLI `--where` won't match arbitrary manifest fields that HTTP/MCP now support.

Trivial fix: delete the local pair and delegate to `ticket_api::query_helpers::apply_field_filters` (as HTTP/MCP do).

### Note on validation
I verified the logic statically; I couldn't independently run the build here (the earlier install-tools.sh failed — environment, not your change), so I'm trusting your reported test passes for the five targeted suites.

### Recommendation
- `7c281aec` (G-B.1) — clean, mergeable.
- `f3a58d3c` — blockers cleared; ready to move to `done`.
- `c680b137` — functionally parity-complete for HTTP/MCP; fold the CLI helper in for true three-transport parity before closing.

Want me to make the one-line CLI delegation fix so `c680b137` is fully parity-clean, and then advance `f3a58d3c` to `done`?

Re-review complete. Both blockers are genuinely resolved; the de-dup is done except one residual I want to flag.

## Re-review verdict

### ✅ Blocker 1 — resolved
The `deny_external_paths` gate around ancestor discovery is gone; the ancestor loop in cross_store_edges.rs is now unconditional. I traced classification across all policy shapes and it's correct:
- `include_ancestors=true, deny_external_paths=false` → ancestor is in `included_store_roots` → **Ok** (no false warning).
- `include_ancestors=true, deny_external_paths=true` (real memory-api) → suppressed from `included` by workspace.rs but added to `discoverable` → **CrossWorkspaceEdge warning**. The 5 root-store danglers now become warnings, not errors — acceptance criterion met.
- unresolvable → **DanglingEdge**. Dead `let _ = root;` replaced with `.any(...)`. Good.

### ✅ Blocker 2 — resolved
The classifier and ticket/spec health regressions now use the production shape `include_ancestors=true, deny_external_paths=true` and assert the ancestor edge yields `cross_workspace_edge` — cross_store_edges.rs. This is the guard that would have caught Blocker 1.

### ✅ De-dup — done (HTTP + MCP)
New query_helpers.rs centralizes the three helpers; HTTP quality.rs and MCP health.rs + server.rs all delegate. Bonus: `apply_field_filters` now reads arbitrary manifest fields (matching `list_extended`), and `resolve_uuid_with_prefix` gained hex validation + case-insensitive `simple()` matching. Solid upgrade over the copy-paste.

### 🟡 Minor residual — CLI still has a third copy
health.rs keeps its own `apply_field_filters` + `matches_filters`, still limited to `state`/`type`/`title`. Two consequences:
- a third duplicate the centralization missed, and
- a re-introduced parity gap: CLI `--where` won't match arbitrary manifest fields that HTTP/MCP now support.

Trivial fix: delete the local pair and delegate to `ticket_api::query_helpers::apply_field_filters` (as HTTP/MCP do).

### Note on validation
I verified the logic statically; I couldn't independently run the build here (the earlier install-tools.sh failed — environment, not your change), so I'm trusting your reported test passes for the five targeted suites.

### Recommendation
- `7c281aec` (G-B.1) — clean, mergeable.
- `f3a58d3c` — blockers cleared; ready to move to `done`.
- `c680b137` — functionally parity-complete for HTTP/MCP; fold the CLI helper in for true three-transport parity before closing.

Committed.

Created commits:
1. `memory-api` commit `17ab1f8`
- Message: `fix(ticket-api): resolve cross-store health classification and transport parity`
- Includes the blocker fixes, shared query helper extraction, and CLI/HTTP/MCP parity wiring.

2. `memory-api` commit `40de7aa`
- Message: `chore(tickets): advance content-materialization ticket states`
- Includes ticket state/history updates for `f3a58d3c`, `c680b137`, and `7c281aec`.

3. Root repo commit `153d21a`
- Message: `chore: update memory-api submodule pointer`
- Updates root to the new `memory-api` submodule SHA.

Post-commit status:
- `memory-api`: clean.
- Root repo: unchanged pre-existing unrelated modifications remain in `.session/...` and dirty `memory-viewers` (untouched).
